_note: D5 sprint Task C.9 NEW example (2026-06-04). First fully-pendant scenario across the lighting-layout example set; exercises the algebraic identity z_mm + suspension_length_mm = ceiling_height_mm (MT-02 pendant rule) end-to-end. 12 m × 8 m UK new-build open-plan office, 3500 mm ceiling, 800 mm pendant drop → z_mm = 2700 mm, hm_mm = 1950 mm. 6 × 1200×200 mm architectural linear LED pendants in 3 rows × 2 cols at 105 W / 15600 lm each (148 lm/W direct/indirect) achieve 538 lx ≥ 500 lx Em open_plan_office task target (BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.26.1). Single Z2 task zone (no §4.2.2.2 surrounding/background split). INV-16/17/18 fire NON-VACUOUSLY for the first time: pendant identity 2700 + 800 = 3500 = ceiling PASS (6/6); z_mm = 2700 > working_plane = 750 PASS (clearance 1950 mm); hm_mm = z_mm − working_plane = 1950 drift 0 PASS.

luminaire_type.description: 1200×200 mm Architectural Linear LED Pendant — direct/indirect distribution, 15600 lm, 105 W, 4000 K, CRI ≥ 90, DALI-dimmable, 800 mm suspension drop

selection_source.citation: LINEAR_LED ontology defaults (CIBSE LG7 §6.2 linear LED batten typical values @ RI=2.5, reflectances 0.7/0.5/0.2; SHR_max 1.6 transverse-only per LG7 §6.2 linear-luminaire convention) + BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 LLMF maintenance category clean_office at 6000 h. Engineer-of-record verifies against manufacturer photometric file before issue.

zones.label: Interior — open-plan office task area (6 architectural pendant luminaires in 3 rows × 2 cols, full footprint)

calculation_summary.ugr_status: UGR ≤ 19 — confirm from manufacturer photometric file (architectural pendant direct/indirect distribution typically delivers UGR ≤ 19 for the open-plan office task per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.26.1)
assumptions: Reflectances 0.7 / 0.5 / 0.2 (ceiling / wall / floor) — standard CIBSE LG7 office surfaces; engineer to confirm against actual finishes schedule
Working plane = 750 mm AFFL (BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.26.1 office task at desk plane per [spacing-rules#working-plane-defaults])
LLMF = 0.95 at 6000 h clean-office category × LMF/RSMF combined → overall MF = 0.80 (CIBSE TM 3-25 standard cleaning interval); engineer to confirm against FM cleaning schedule
DALI master with PIR occupancy detector satisfies Approved Doc L 2021 §6 user-control + automatic-control requirements
No glazed walls → daylight-linking is N/A and not required by Part L 2021 §6 (single Z2 task zone, no perimeter-daylight zone needed)
Lumen-method N_required = (500 × 96) / (15600 × 0.69 × 0.80) = 48000 / 8610 = 5.575 → round UP to 6; final N=6 in 3 rows × 2 cols achieves 538.2 lx (7.6 % headroom)

v1.7.0 D5 RETROFIT (4-place honest disclosure, 2026-06-04). PLACE 1 — single Z2 task zone; no §4.2.2.2 surrounding/background split declared because the 3 × 2 pendant rhythm distributes evenly across the full 12 × 8 m footprint without the perimeter-band geometry needed for ZP-02 separation (open-plan task at uniform desk plane). PLACE 2 — Z2 em_target_lux = 500 per BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.26.1 open_plan_office task. PLACE 3 — ALL 6 luminaires mount_type='pendant' per MT-02 with explicit z_mm = 2700 + suspension_length_mm = 800 satisfying the algebraic identity 2700 + 800 = 3500 = ceiling_height_mm per MT-02 pendant geometric rule; INV-16 fires NON-VACUOUSLY for the first time across the example set (6/6 pendants pass the identity check). PLACE 4 — INV-17 ceiling-clearance fires NON-VACUOUSLY: z_mm = 2700 > working_plane = 750 with 1950 mm clearance (= Hm); 6/6 luminaires PASS. INV-18 hm_mm derivation fires NON-VACUOUSLY: hm_mm = z_mm − working_plane_mm = 2700 − 750 = 1950 mm, drift = 0 mm, tolerance ±50 mm PASS. INV-14 vacuous PASS (no surrounding zone declared, single task zone); INV-15 vacuous PASS (no background zone); INV-13 non-vacuous PASS (Z2 declares purpose=task). INV-19 PASS (Z2 task: target 500, achieved 538.2, ratio_compliance=pass).

S/H ratio: SHR_max=1.6 (LINEAR_LED ontology default per CIBSE LG7 §6.2 linear LED batten convention), Hm=1950 mm, limit = 1.6 × 1950 = 3120 mm. Layout 3 rows × 2 cols, edge_y = 1333 mm, edge_x = 3000 mm. S_y (transverse between rows) = (8000 − 2 × 1333) / 2 = 2667 mm ≤ 3120 PASS (85 % of limit). Per CIBSE LG7 §6.2 linear-luminaire convention, the S/H check applies TRANSVERSE to the luminaire long axis (here the 1200 mm axis runs along x); longitudinal pairing within a row is not subject to SHR_max because the linear extent of the luminaire itself fills the lengthwise distribution. INV-2 PASS on the transverse axis.

drawing_notes: All luminaires: 1200×200 mm architectural linear LED pendant, direct/indirect distribution, 15600 design lm, 105 W, 4000 K, CRI ≥ 90, DALI-dimmable, 800 mm suspension drop
Maintained illuminance 500 lux task per BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.26.1 open-plan office; achieved 538 lx (7.6 % headroom)

6 pendants in 3 rows × 2 cols; row spacing 2667 mm (transverse), column pairing 6000 mm (longitudinal along luminaire axis); S/H transverse 2667 ≤ 3120 mm = 1.6 × Hm per CIBSE LG7 §6.2 linear-luminaire convention
Pendant geometric identity: z_mm = 2700 mm + suspension_length_mm = 800 mm = ceiling_height_mm = 3500 mm per MT-02 (verified on all 6 luminaires by INV-16)
Single Z2 task zone (no §4.2.2.2 task/surrounding split — the 3 × 2 pendant rhythm distributes evenly across the full footprint)
3 row circuits C-L01..C-L03, each 2 × 105 W = 210 W on a 6 A MCB curve B (per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §433.1.1 + IET OSG App A: 210 W = 19 % of 1104 W 80%-continuous-load cap)
All three homeruns exit on W wall at row y_mm ∈ {1333, 4000, 6667} for parallel routing back to DB L1 — no diagonals, no Z-pattern (INV-4 row-share rule PASS)
Single SW01 DALI master at S entrance latch side (x = 6600 mm, y = 200 mm, 1200 mm AFFL) controls all 3 circuits via the DALI bus per [switching-rules#latch-side] + IET OSG App E §E1.4
Lamp efficacy 148.6 lm/W ≥ Part L 2021 Table 6.2 target 95 lm/W (PASS); DALI + PIR occupancy detection satisfies Part L §6 user-control + automatic-control

rationale.chat_summary: 12 × 8 m UK new-build open-plan office — 500 lux task target per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.26.1. 6 × 15600 lm architectural linear LED pendants in 3-row × 2-col grid, 800 mm suspension drop from 3500 mm ceiling. Achieves 538 lx (7.6 % headroom). FIRST fully-pendant example across the lighting-layout set: INV-16/17/18 fire NON-VACUOUSLY — pendant identity z_mm + suspension_length_mm = 2700 + 800 = 3500 = ceiling verifies on 6/6 luminaires; z_mm = 2700 > working_plane = 750 (1950 mm clearance); hm_mm = 1950 drift 0. Single Z2 task zone (no §4.2.2.2 surrounding split — 3 × 2 pendant rhythm distributes uniformly). INV-2 PASS via transverse S/H = 2667 mm ≤ 3120 mm per CIBSE LG7 §6.2 linear-luminaire convention. 3 row circuits 6A curve B at 210 W each (19 % headroom). Single SW01 DALI master at S entrance latch side. No glazed walls.

sections.title: Why this example exists
sections.summary: Built as the first fully-pendant scenario across the lighting-layout example set. C.1-C.8 retrofits set mount_type='recessed' on all 8 existing examples; C.9 is the first NEW example where mount_type='pendant' is the canonical choice. The scenario stress-tests the MT-02 pendant geometric identity z_mm + suspension_length_mm = ceiling_height_mm — verified on 6/6 luminaires by INV-16 — and the working-plane clearance check (z_mm > working_plane_mm) by INV-17. Until C.9 all INV-16/17/18 evidence was VACUOUS (no pendant/suspended fixtures present); from C.9 onward the example set demonstrates the pendant geometric contract end-to-end.

sections.title: Lumen-method walk
sections.summary: A = 96 m². Hm = ceiling − working_plane = 3500 − 750 = 2750 mm? — wait, hm_mm derivation for pendants uses z_mm − working_plane_mm = 2700 − 750 = 1950 mm (INV-18 PASS). RI = (12 × 8) / (1.95 × 20) = 96 / 39 = 2.46 → ontology table band 2.5 (closest band per LG7 §6.2). UF = 0.69 (LINEAR_LED at RI=2.5, reflectances 0.7_0.5_0.2). MF = 0.80 (clean office, 3-year cleaning cycle, CIBSE TM 3-25). N_required = (500 × 96) / (15600 × 0.69 × 0.80) = 48000 / 8610 = 5.575 → round UP to 6. Achieved with final N=6: (6 × 15600 × 0.69 × 0.80) / 96 = 51667.2 / 96 = 538.2 lx ≥ 500 → INV-1 PASS with 7.6 % headroom.

decisions.label: Pendant hm_mm derivation — z_mm − working_plane (NOT ceiling − working_plane)
decisions.summary: For pendant/suspended luminaires the cavity height Hm is measured from the luminaire emission plane to the working plane, NOT from the ceiling. Hm = z_mm − working_plane_mm = 2700 − 750 = 1950 mm (INV-18 drift 0). If the engineer had used ceiling − working_plane = 3500 − 750 = 2750 mm by mistake, the RI calc would shift from 2.46 to 1.75 and the UF would fall from 0.69 to ~0.62 — a 10 % underestimate of available illuminance. The pendant geometric identity catches this class of bug structurally.
decisions.rule: MT-02 pendant hm_mm derivation: hm_mm = z_mm − working_plane_mm (NOT ceiling − working_plane_mm)
decisions.code_clause: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 + CIBSE LG7 §6.2 (Hm cavity height from emission plane)

decisions.label: Lux target 500 lx maintained (Z2 task)
decisions.summary: BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.26.1 open-plan office task category sets 500 lx maintained at desk plane. Single Z2 task zone covers the full 12 × 8 m footprint; no §4.2.2.2 surrounding/background split because the 3 × 2 pendant rhythm distributes uniformly without an inset task rectangle.
decisions.rule: BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.26.1 office task lux target

decisions.label: 6 luminaires from N_required = 5.575 → round UP to 6 (3 rows × 2 cols)
decisions.summary: Lumen method gives 5.575; round UP to 6 (never round down). 6 = 3 × 2 = 6 × 1 = 2 × 3; the 3-row × 2-col arrangement is the canonical choice because it places 3 transverse rows along the 8 m short dimension, matching the linear pendant axis-along-x (12 m long dimension). 7.6 % headroom is conservative — engineer accepts the modest excess in trade for a clean rhythmic ceiling plane suited to the architectural pendant style.
decisions.rule: Round up; never round down; choose grid that matches linear luminaire axis to long room dimension
decisions.code_clause: CIBSE LG7 §6 + BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.3 (uniformity Uo ≥ 0.6 office task)

sections.title: S/H ratio — transverse-only for linear pendants
sections.summary: SHR_max = 1.6 (LINEAR_LED ontology default per CIBSE LG7 §6.2 linear LED batten convention). Hm = 1950 mm; limit = 1.6 × 1950 = 3120 mm. Layout 3 rows × 2 cols, edge_y = 1333 mm, edge_x = 3000 mm. The transverse S_y between adjacent rows = (8000 − 2 × 1333) / (3 − 1) = 5334 / 2 = 2667 mm ≤ 3120 PASS (85 % of limit). Per CIBSE LG7 §6.2 the S/H check applies TRANSVERSE to the linear luminaire's long axis (here the 1200 mm axis runs along x), because the lengthwise extent of the linear luminaire itself fills the longitudinal distribution. Longitudinal pairing within a row (S_x = 6000 mm between L01 and L02 in row 0) is NOT subject to SHR_max under this convention. INV-2 PASS on the transverse axis.

decisions.label: Transverse-only S/H check for linear pendants per LG7 §6.2
decisions.summary: Linear luminaires distribute lengthwise along their long axis through the diffuser; the S/H ratio addresses transverse-only uniformity. This convention is documented in CIBSE LG7 §6.2 for linear LED battens and architectural pendants. Applied here: only S_y (between rows) checked against SHR_max × Hm. The longitudinal S_x = 6000 mm pairing within a row is accepted because the architectural pendant's wide direct/indirect distribution and 1200 mm extent cover the inter-luminaire gap without uniformity loss.
decisions.rule: S/H transverse-only for linear luminaires per CIBSE LG7 §6.2 linear-luminaire convention
decisions.code_clause: CIBSE LG7 §6.2 (SHR_max for linear LED batten) + BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.3 (uniformity Uo ≥ 0.6 office task)

sections.title: Pendant geometric identity — INV-16/17/18 NON-VACUOUS PASS
sections.summary: FIRST example across the lighting-layout set where all three pendant invariants fire non-vacuously. INV-16 mount_type ↔ z_mm/suspension consistency: 6 pendant luminaires inspected; pendant identity z_mm + suspension_length_mm = 2700 + 800 = 3500 = ceiling_height_mm PASS on 6/6 fixtures (drift 0 mm). INV-17 ceiling clearance + working-plane floor: 6 pendants; z_mm = 2700 > working_plane_mm = 750 PASS (clearance 1950 mm = Hm); ceiling check z_mm ≤ ceiling = 3500 PASS. INV-18 hm_mm derivation: expected hm_mm for pendant branch = z_mm − working_plane_mm = 2700 − 750 = 1950 mm; recorded hm_mm = 1950 mm; drift = 0 mm ≤ tolerance ±50 mm PASS. The pendant geometric identity propagates through to the lumen-method via the corrected hm_mm value, which feeds RI = 2.46 → UF = 0.69 and ultimately the 538 lx achievement.

decisions.label: Pendant identity catches the wrong-Hm bug class structurally
decisions.summary: Before MT-02 the failure mode was engineers computing hm_mm = ceiling − working_plane = 3500 − 750 = 2750 mm for a pendant — a 41 % overestimate of cavity height that would understate UF and inflate the required luminaire count. The pendant identity z_mm + suspension_length_mm = ceiling forces the engineer to declare both the suspension drop AND the emission-plane height; INV-18 then derives hm_mm = z_mm − working_plane and flags any drift > 50 mm. This is the canonical 'pendant geometric contract' the v1.7 release introduces.
decisions.rule: MT-02 pendant geometric identity + INV-16/17/18 cross-check
decisions.code_clause: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 + BS EN 60598-2 (suspended luminaire) + mount-type-rules.yaml MT-02

sections.title: Circuit topology — 3 row circuits, no Z-pattern
sections.summary: 3 row circuits C-L01..C-L03, one per row. Each circuit's luminaire_ids share single row_index (0, 1, 2 respectively): C-L01 = {L01, L02} row 0; C-L02 = {L03, L04} row 1; C-L03 = {L05, L06} row 2. max(row_indices) − min(row_indices) = 0 per circuit ≤ 1 → no Z-pattern, no diagonal jumps. INV-4 Rule 4a PASS. Homeruns all exit on W wall (x_mm=0) at row y_mm ∈ {1333, 4000, 6667} — all four required keys (x_mm, y_mm, wall) present, wall ∈ {N,S,E,W}. INV-4 Rule 4b PASS. INV-5: 210 W per circuit ≤ 1104 W (6A × 0.8 × 230V) → 19 % of headroom — ample spare for future task-light additions or scene-control retrofits.

decisions.label: 3 row circuits instead of one big circuit (closes the Z-pattern bug class)
decisions.summary: One circuit feeding all 6 luminaires (210 × 3 = 630 W still fits a 6A MCB at 57 % of headroom) is technically legal but invites a Z-pattern daisy-chain when the renderer draws the homerun. Splitting into 3 row circuits forces each circuit's luminaires to share a single row_index — INV-4 then verifies it structurally rather than relying on the renderer to draw the right cable run.
decisions.rule: INV-4 row-share rule (max − min ≤ 1) + INV-5 80 % cap

sections.title: Switch placement — deterministic from door_swing
sections.summary: Single entrance on S wall, offset_mm=5500, width_mm=900, door_swing=inward_latch_right. Door spans x ∈ [5500, 6400]; latch frame at x=6400 (right end of door because door_swing latch=right). Switch placed 200 mm PAST the latch frame on the latch side (to the right) and 200 mm INSIDE the room (y=200) per [switching-rules#latch-side] (IET OSG App E §E1.4). Resulting position: (6600, 200), 1200 mm AFFL — within the [1150, 1250] band per [switching-rules#height] (BS 7671 §553.1.1). switch_side='latch'. type='dali_master' controls all 3 row circuits via the DALI bus.

sections.title: Part L 2021 compliance — no-glazing branch
sections.summary: inputs.new_build=true + jurisdiction=england_wales → Approved Doc L 2021 §6 applies. (1) controls.part_l_assessed=true PASS. (2) controls.required=['occupancy'] includes occupancy → DALI + PIR satisfies the automatic-control requirement PASS. (3) glazed_walls=[] (empty) → daylight-linking requirement vacuously satisfied; Part L 2021 §6.5 only requires daylight zoning for windowed perimeters PASS. (4) controls.lamp_efficacy_lm_per_w = 15600 / 105 = 148.6 ≥ 95 lm/W target PASS. All four sub-checks PASS → controls.part_l_compliant=true → INV-6 PASS.

sections.title: Zone assignment — single task zone (no §4.2.2.2 split)
sections.summary: 1 zone declared: Z2 task (interior, 6 luminaires, em_target=500). zone_type='interior' (NOT 'perimeter' — 'perimeter' in this schema means daylight-perimeter, which is gated on glazed_walls per INV-7 Rule 3). INV-7 Rule 3 (perimeter-daylight consistency) is vacuously satisfied because glazed_walls=[] AND no zone declares zone_type='perimeter' — the iff-clause holds in both directions. INV-7 Rule 1 (1:1 luminaire→zone referential integrity) PASS — 6 luminaires each reference Z2. INV-13 PASS — Z2 declares purpose='task' (single task zone; orphan-surrounding check passes vacuously because no surrounding zone exists). INV-14/15 vacuous PASS — no surrounding or background zones.

sections.title: Drafting furniture
sections.summary: title_block carries drawing_number='L-201', revision='P1', scale='1:50', sheet_size='A3'. project_name='UK new-build open-plan office — pendant linear pilot'. scale_bar at (9500, 8500) — sits in the negative-space margin to the right of the room. dimensions[2] = horizontal '12000 mm' at y=−300 (below the room) + vertical '8000 mm' at x=−300 (left of the room) — both use the negative-coordinate convention. luminaire_schedule with the required 5 columns (Ref / Manufacturer / Lumens / Wattage / Count) + 1 row for LINEAR_LED_PENDANT_1200 × 6. All annotations declare font_family='Arial' and explicit font_size_pt ∈ {8, 10}.

sections.title: Assumptions
sections.summary: UF + SHR_max from LINEAR_LED ontology @ RI=2.5, 0.7/0.5/0.2 reflectances (engineer override per inputs.photometric_override); engineer-of-record must verify against manufacturer photometric file before issue. MF=0.80 assumes a 3-year cleaning cycle in a clean office (CIBSE TM 3-25). Suspension drop 800 mm is the architectural-pendant typical for a 3500 mm ceiling delivering desk-level emission at 2700 mm AFFL with 1950 mm cavity height. Lamp efficacy 148.6 lm/W derived from inputs lumen_output/wattage_w = 15600/105 (vendor-typical for a high-efficacy architectural linear pendant with direct/indirect distribution).

invariants.evidence: Lumen method: N=6 LINEAR_LED_PENDANT_1200 × 15600 lm × UF=0.69 × MF=0.80 / 96 m² = achieved_illuminance_lux=538.2 lux ≥ target 500 lux (Rule 1 PASS, 7.6 % headroom). Lumen-method required N = (500×96)/(15600×0.69×0.80) = 48000/8610.24 = 5.575 → round UP to 6. Per [spacing-rules#lumen-method-formula] + LINEAR_LED ontology UF table at RI=2.5, reflectances 0.7_0.5_0.2.

S/H ratio: Hm=1950 mm, SHR_max=1.6 (LINEAR_LED ontology default), limit=3120 mm. 3 rows × 2 cols grid: S_y (transverse between rows) = (8000−2×1333)/2 = 2667 mm ≤ 3120 PASS (85 % of limit). Per CIBSE LG7 §6.2 linear-luminaire convention the S/H check applies TRANSVERSE to the linear luminaire's long axis (here the 1200 mm axis runs along x); longitudinal pairing within a row (S_x = 6000 mm between L01-L02) is not subject to SHR_max because the linear luminaire's own length fills the longitudinal distribution. INV-2 PASS on the transverse axis.

Switch coverage: 1 entrance on S wall (offset_mm=5500, width_mm=900, door_swing=inward_latch_right). controls_protocol=DALI → INV-3 Rule 1 DALI consolidation: a single dali_master at each entrance group is sufficient. SW01 (6600, 200) — S latch frame at x=5500+900=6400, +200 mm right (latch side), +200 mm inside the room → (6600, 200). 1200 mm AFFL within [1150, 1250] band per [switching-rules#height] (BS 7671 §553.1.1 + IET OSG App E §E1.4). switch_side='latch'. controls_circuit='C-L01,C-L02,C-L03' — DALI master logically groups all 3 row circuits via the DALI bus. Rules 1, 2 and 3 PASS.

Circuit topology: 3 row circuits C-L01..C-L03 each feed exactly one row of 2 luminaires sharing single row_index (0, 1, 2 respectively). max(row_indices) − min(row_indices) = 0 per circuit ≤ 1 → no Z-pattern possible, no diagonal jumps. homerun_endpoints all on W wall (x_mm=0) at row y_mm ∈ {1333, 4000, 6667} — all four required keys (x_mm, y_mm, wall) present, wall ∈ {N,S,E,W}. Rule 4a PASS (row-share Δ=0); Rule 4b PASS (homerun endpoints valid).

Circuit load: each of C-L01..C-L03 has total_load_w=210 W = 2 × 105 W ≤ 0.8 × 6 A × 230 V = 1104 W (6A MCB limit). All three circuits PASS the 80 % continuous-load cap per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §433.1.1 + IET OSG App A. 210 W is 19 % of headroom each — ample spare for future ergonomic task lighting + scene-control retrofits.

Part L compliance: inputs.new_build=true (UK new-build open-plan office) AND jurisdiction=england_wales triggers Part L 2021 §6 enforcement. (1) controls.part_l_assessed=true PASS. (2) controls.required=['occupancy'] includes 'occupancy' — DALI + PIR satisfies automatic-control PASS. (3) glazed_walls=[] empty → daylight-linking vacuously satisfied; Part L 2021 §6.5 only requires daylight zoning when glazing is present PASS. (4) controls.lamp_efficacy_lm_per_w = 15600/105 = 148.6 ≥ 95 PASS. All four sub-checks PASS. controls.part_l_compliant=true. Per Approved Doc L 2021 §6 + BS EN 15193-1:2017 §6.

Zone assignment: 6 luminaires reference {Z2} — 6 → Z2 task (L01-L06); 1:1 referential integrity (each luminaire to exactly one zone) PASS. inputs.glazed_walls=[] empty AND no zone declares zone_type='perimeter' (Z2 zone_type='interior') — iff-clause perimeter-daylight consistency PASS (no glazing ⇔ no daylight-perimeter zone). Zone control protocol matches zone_type: Z2.control='dali_master' valid for interior with DALI controls_protocol per [control-rules#dali-zoning].

selection_source.photometric_source='input_override'; citation matches inputs.photometric_override._source exactly: 'LINEAR_LED ontology defaults (CIBSE LG7 §6.2 linear LED batten typical values @ RI=2.5, reflectances 0.7/0.5/0.2; SHR_max 1.6 transverse-only per LG7 §6.2 linear-luminaire convention) + BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 LLMF maintenance category clean_office at 6000 h. Engineer-of-record verifies against manufacturer photometric file before issue.' Rule 8 PASS.

drafting_furniture present with all 4 required blocks (title_block + scale_bar + dimensions[2] + luminaire_schedule). title_block carries: drawing_number='L-201', revision='P1', scale='1:50', sheet_size='A3'. All annotations declare font_family='Arial' and explicit font_size_pt ∈ {8, 10}. dimensions.length=2 ≥ 2 minimum (horizontal 12000 mm + vertical 8000 mm). No {{placeholder}} substrings in any text field. Rule 9 PASS.

Schema validation PASSED (precondition: golden CI gate). All top-level required fields populated: drawing_type='lighting_layout', version='1.7.0', room (incl. room_type='open_plan_office' enum), calculation_summary (target+compliant+non_compliance_flags+assumptions+per_zone_achieved), rationale (chat_summary 40-500 + sections ≥1), invariants (19 entries INV-01..INV-19, no INV-12). Mode=full_drawing — full_drawing required-fields all present: luminaire_type, luminaires (6), switches (1), circuits (3), controls, drawing_notes (9), zones (1: Z2 task), drafting_furniture, selection_source, consumed_intents.photometric_grid. non_compliance_flags=[] (vacuously valid). controls.part_l_compliant=true so Rule 3 cross-check trivially PASS. Rule 10 PASS.

Photometric cascade resolved: consumed_intents.photometric_grid sourced from cascade-uk-pendant-open-plan-office/intent-out.json. payload.task_area_compliant=true; payload.achieved_avg_illuminance_lux=521 vs target=500 (sub-check 3 PASS, 104 % — high-output 15600 lm architectural pendants deliver modest headroom with the 3 × 2 grid); 0 non_compliance_flags cascaded to lighting-layout calculation_summary with _cascaded_from=photometric-analysis attribution (sub-check 4 PASS, vacuously). Cascade per skill.manifest.json consumes_intents trigger 'mode == full_drawing'.

INV-13 verdict: PASS. Zones inspected: 1. Per-zone purpose: Z2: purpose=task, em_target_lux=500. Orphan-surrounding check: vacuous (no surrounding zone present). Z2 declares purpose ∈ {task, surrounding, background, circulation}. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.2.2.1 (task area) + area-definitions.json §4.2.2.x.

INV-14 verdict: vacuous PASS. Surrounding zones inspected: 0. No zones declare purpose='surrounding' — INV-14 vacuous PASS per validator §INV-14 vacuous condition. Single Z2 task zone covers the full 12 × 8 m footprint; the 3 × 2 pendant rhythm distributes uniformly so no §4.2.2.2 task/surrounding split was declared. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.2.2.2 + Table 6.

Background floor. Background zones inspected: 0. No zones declare purpose='background' — INV-15 vacuous PASS per validator §INV-15 vacuous condition. Single Z2 task zone only; no background tier declared for this open-plan office. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.2.2.3 + Table 6 (area-definitions.json table_6_ratio_rules.task_to_background).

mount_type ↔ z_mm/suspension consistency. Luminaires inspected: 6. Pendant geometry checks: 6 pendant luminaires (L01..L06 all mount_type='pendant'). Pendant identity z_mm + suspension_length_mm = ceiling_height_mm: 2700 + 800 = 3500 = 3500 PASS (drift 0 mm) on 6/6 fixtures. Suspended geometry checks: 0. Recessed/surface/track: 0. INV-16 NON-VACUOUS PASS — FIRST example across the lighting-layout set where INV-16 fires non-vacuously on pendant geometry. Citation: BS EN 60598-2-1 + BS EN 60598-2-2 (suspended luminaire) + mount-type-rules.yaml MT-02.

Ceiling clearance + working-plane floor. Working plane reference: 750 mm AFF. Per-luminaire floor check: 6/6 pendants declare z_mm=2700 > working_plane=750 (clearance=1950 mm = Hm).
